meaning: adj.智力迟钝的,发展迟缓的
examples: Last but not least, educators recognise that all students are unique and have different interests and abilities which can be retarded if they have to study all subjects.
The progression of the disease can be retarded by early surgery.
The high costs of converting to sound and the early limitations of sound technology were among the factors that suppressed innovations or retarded advancement in these other areas.
If Mr Ellis's bill passes, it will commute all death sentences already passed on mentally retarded prisoners.
According to one opinion poll, although 81% of Texans support capital punishment, 73% oppose the execution of the mentally retarded.
